---
title: "Bingjin (Ben) Xue"
page-layout: full
pagetitle: "Bingjin (Ben) Xue — Economist"
include-in-header:
  - text: |
      <meta name="description" content="Bingjin Xue (Ben Xue) — Assistant Professor of Economics at the University of New Hampshire. Studies how policy shapes the health, work, and wellbeing of vulnerable populations, using causal inference and machine learning on large administrative data.">
      <script type="application/ld+json">
      {
        "@context": "https://schema.org",
        "@type": "Person",
        "name": "Bingjin Xue",
        "alternateName": ["Ben Xue", "Bingjin (Ben) Xue"],
        "givenName": "Bingjin",
        "additionalName": "Ben",
        "familyName": "Xue",
        "jobTitle": "Assistant Professor of Economics",
        "affiliation": {
          "@type": "CollegeOrUniversity",
          "name": "University of New Hampshire",
          "department": "Peter T. Paul College of Business and Economics"
        },
        "email": "bingjin.xue@unh.edu",
        "url": "https://bingjinxue.github.io/personal_web/",
        "sameAs": [
          "https://scholar.google.com/citations?hl=en&user=GjuByKMAAAAJ",
          "https://github.com/bingjinxue",
          "https://www.linkedin.com/in/xuebingjin/"
        ]
      }
      </script>
---

::: {.home-hero}

![](assets/photo.jpg)

<div class="bio">

<p>
I am an Assistant Professor of Economics at the University of New Hampshire. I study how policy shapes the health, work, and wellbeing of vulnerable populations — combining causal inference and machine learning with large administrative data. My recent work asks how naloxone access laws change maternal and infant health, how abortion restrictions ripple into economic hardship and crime, and how the technology hospitals adopt reshapes the specialists patients are sent to. The thread connecting them: the people most affected by a policy are often the least visible in the data.
</p>

<div class="links">
[Email](mailto:bingjin.xue@unh.edu) ·
[CV](assets/Bingjin Xue CV.pdf){target="_blank"} ·
[Google Scholar](https://scholar.google.com/citations?hl=en&user=GjuByKMAAAAJ) ·
[GitHub](https://github.com/bingjinxue) ·
[LinkedIn](https://www.linkedin.com/in/xuebingjin/) .
</div>

</div>
:::
